front: Play: ends with Agate shouting "WE'RE STORMBIRDS OF THE WORKING-CLASS" as cab drivers chant "STRIKE!"
Play: Lefty's body is found at the end, prompting the strike chant
Play: seven vignettes separated by blackouts
Play: Miller punches Fayette after refusing to spy on Dr. Brenner
Play: Florrie says "every princess in the Balkans is wearing a dress like this" when Sid picks her up
Play: a stenographer tells the actor Phil that "one dollar buys nine loaves of bread and one copy of The Communist Manifesto"
Play: Berger family in the Bronx — Jacob jumps off the roof after Bessie smashes his Caruso records
Play: Hennie runs off with Moe Axelrod at the end
Play: Jacob advises his grandson to "fight so life shouldn't be printed on dollar bills"
Play: character proclaims after his grandfather's death, "I was born! I swear to God, I'm one week old!"
Play: Bessie forces Hennie to marry the immigrant boarder Sam
Play: a woman tells her husband their daughter has never even seen a grapefruit
Play: Joe Bonaparte breaks his hand, ruining his prospects as a violinist
Play: Cleo Singer says she is looking for "a whole full world, with all the trimmings"
Play: about anti-Nazi resistance; the Taussig brothers print leaflets about an American embargo
back: Till the Day I Die (Clifford Odets)

front: Play: contains the first use of the phrase "male chauvinism"
back: Till the Day I Die (Clifford Odets)

front: Playwright: member of the Group Theatre
Playwright: named names to HUAC with Elia Kazan
Playwright: the Coen brothers modeled Barton Fink on this playwright
Playwright: co-wrote the screenplay for Sweet Smell of Success
Playwright: controversially lost the 1935 Pulitzer to Zoe Akins's The Old Maid
